info.name: Ocean Node API
info.description:
Complete collection of HTTP endpoints exposed by an Ocean Node's HTTP server.

Set the `baseUrl` collection variable to your node (default `http://localhost:8000`). Many endpoints are authenticated with a signature over `consumerAddress` + `nonce` + `command`, or with an auth-token `Authorization` header. Convenience variables (`consumerAddress`, `signature`, `nonce`, `chainId`, `did`, `serviceId`, `jobId`, `token`, `bucketId`, `wallet`, `owner`) are provided as placeholders.

Generated from the route definitions in `src/components/httpRoutes/` and their handler implementations.

request.description: Returns node identity and the list of available service endpoints (nodeId, chainIds, providerAddress, nodePublicKey, serviceEndpoints, software, version).
Retrieve the full DDO for a DID. Append an optional `/true` path segment to force a fresh lookup.
Retrieve DDO metadata for a DID. Optional trailing `/true` forces a fresh lookup.
Query indexed assets. Body is a search query object (filter/query/sort/from/size).
Query DDO state by `did`, `nft`, or `txId` (at least one required).
Validate a DDO's schema and signature before publishing.
Get the current nonce for a user address (used when signing requests).
Get the fees required to access an asset service.
ECIES-encrypt the raw request body. Returns application/octet-stream. 25MB limit.
Encrypt a file (AES or ECIES). Accepts a StorageObject JSON, raw binary, or multipart. Returns encrypted bytes with X-Encrypted-By / X-Encrypted-Method headers.
Decrypt a DDO document. Returns the decrypted payload as text/plain.
Download an asset file after a valid transfer (order). Streams the file.
Get file info for an asset (by `did`+`serviceId`) or by a raw file `type` descriptor. `consumerAddress` is required for NODE_PERSISTENT_STORAGE ACL gating.
List available compute environments (optionally filtered by chain).
Validate algorithm + datasets and return a price/initialization quote.
Start a paid compute job. Optional fields: policyServer, metadata, additionalViewers, queueMaxWaitTime, encryptedDockerRegistryAuth, output, outputBucketId.
Start a free compute job (no payment) on a free-tier environment.
Get the status of a compute job.
Stop a running compute job (parameters are query strings).
Download a compute job result by index. Streams the result file.
Stream real-time logs from a running compute job (404 if not running).
List operator-published service templates (sanitized). Not authenticated.
Launch a long-running service container, paid via escrow. Optional: checksum, dockerfile, additionalDockerFiles, dockerCmd, dockerEntrypoint. Services must listen on a high port (>1024).
Read service status/endpoints. Authenticated and owner-scoped. Omit serviceId to list all owned services.
Pay to push the service expiry further out (additionalDuration in seconds, must be positive).
Recreate the service container (no extra charge), keeping the same hostPort and expiresAt. Optional userData replaces stored env vars.
Tear down the service container and network and release resources. Owner-gated.

query.description: Optional: Unix timestamp (seconds) or relative duration like 30s/45m/2h/7d. Omit for full history + live follow.

request.description: Stream real-time stdout/stderr logs from a service container. Authenticated and owner-scoped (401 if consumerAddress is not the owner). Available while Running or Error; 404 otherwise. Optional `since` skips history and starts from a recent point (Unix timestamp or relative duration e.g. 1h).
Create a new persistent storage bucket owned by consumerAddress.
Update a bucket (e.g. set its label).
List buckets for an owner (filtered by access lists for the calling consumer).
List all files in a bucket.
Get the file object metadata for a file in a bucket.
Upload a file to a bucket. Body is the raw file bytes (supports chunked uploads).
Delete a file from a bucket. Returns { success: true }.
Query escrow contract events with optional filters (eventType e.g. Lock/Claimed/Canceled/Deposit/Withdraw/Auth).
Search access lists that include a given wallet address.
Get a specific access list by chainId and contract address.
Create an auth token (returned token can be used as the Authorization header on authenticated routes).
Invalidate an existing auth token.
Pass arbitrary data through to the configured policy server. Streams the response.
Initialize a policy server verification flow. Streams the response.
Fetch the node configuration (admin-only). Note: this GET reads auth fields from the JSON body.
Update the node configuration (admin-only).
Get the current indexing queue.
Get the indexer job pool for a given job identifier.
Get P2P network statistics.
List all connected P2P peers.
Get details for a specific peer by peerId.
Find a peer's multiaddress by peerId.
Get the providers (peers) that serve a single string/CID.
Batch lookup: get providers for an array of strings/CIDs. Body is a JSON array of strings.
Retrieve node logs (admin auth via signed body). Filter/paginate with query params.
Retrieve a single log entry by id (logId in body must match the :id path param).

item.name: Direct Command (P2P)
item.description: POST /directCommand is a single endpoint that dispatches to a protocol command handler chosen by the `command` field. It can run locally or be forwarded to another peer via `node`/`multiAddrs`. Below are representative command bodies; any PROTOCOL_COMMANDS value is accepted (download, encrypt, encryptFile, decryptDDO, getDDO, query, nonce, status, detailedStatus, findDDO, getFees, fileInfo, validateDDO, getComputeEnvironments, startCompute, freeStartCompute, stopCompute, getComputeStatus, getComputeStreamableLogs, getComputeResult, initializeCompute, reindexTx, reindexChain, collectFees, PolicyServerPassthrough, getP2PPeer(s), getP2PNetworkStats, findPeer, createAuthToken, invalidateAuthToken, fetchConfig, pushConfig, getLogs, jobs, persistentStorage*, getAccessList, searchAccessList, getEscrowEvents, serviceGetTemplates, serviceStart, serviceStop, serviceRestart, serviceGetStatus, serviceExtend, serviceGetStreamableLogs, stopNode, ...).

request.description: Fetch a DDO by id over the command interface.
Get the nonce for an address.
Get node status. Use "command": "detailedStatus" for the extended report.
Query indexed assets.
Get the provider fees for an asset service.
Get file info for an asset or a raw file descriptor (type/url/...).
Download an asset file (streams the response).
Encrypt a blob (AES or ECIES).
Decrypt a DDO document.
Find which peers can serve a DDO.
Validate a DDO.
List compute environments over the command interface.
Query escrow events over the command interface.
